title: Design System: abijith.sh — Ink & Clay
colorReference: Muji production catalog, 1986 — unbleached paper stock, deep red-clay ink stamps, everything functional, nothing decorative.

clay.description: Fired terracotta / red ochre pigment. The committed accent color. Covers ~40% of identity surfaces and interactive states.

unbleached.description: Light background. Barely-warm off-white, like quality unbleached paper stock.

inkstone.description: Warm near-black. Clay-tinted printing ink. The darkest light-mode value.

washi.description: Card and secondary surfaces in light mode. One step deeper than Unbleached.

pith.description: Popover and hover surfaces. Deepest light neutral.

sandstone.description: Muted text in light mode. Warm stone gray. Passes WCAG AA (4.7:1 on Unbleached).

linen-edge.description: Borders and dividers in light mode. Warm, visible, not harsh.

kiln-red.description: Destructive / error states in light mode. Pure cardinal red, distinguishable from Clay (H=18 vs H=30).

ironwood.description: Dark background. Warm amber-shifted near-black, like aged ironwood or dark walnut at night.

workshop.description: Card and secondary surfaces in dark mode. Slightly elevated warm surface.

workbench.description: Popover and deeper surfaces in dark mode.

warm-stone.description: Muted text in dark mode. Warm stone. Passes WCAG AA (4.7:1 on Ironwood).

iron-edge.description: Borders in dark mode. Warm, just visible against Ironwood.

ember-red.description: Destructive / error states in dark mode.

hoverLift.note: The only shadow in the system. Applied exclusively on interactive hover feedback.
